cases.rules_text:
1. Prohibited airside entry is present if any one of the following occurs: a person enters without a badge, uses an expired airport badge, or follows a service vehicle through the gate.
2. Restricted airside status is present when the area is marked restricted and no escort officer is present.
3. Runway-area drone operation is present when a person either launches a drone near the runway or pilots one above a service road.
4. Active runway risk is present when the runway lights are active.
5. Signal-interference conduct is present when a person either transmits an unlicensed beacon or jams a tower channel.
6. An aviation warning effect is present when a pilot warning is received.
7. Maintenance authorization records are complete when there is a work order, the person stays within the assigned zone, and exit is logged before closure.
8. Emergency tarmac records are complete when a medical diversion is announced, the rescue route is followed, and the patient transfer form is carried.
9. An airside access violation is established by prohibited airside entry into a restricted airside area.
10. Runway drone interference is established when an airside access violation is combined with runway-area drone operation and active runway risk.
11. Navigation signal interference is established when signal-interference conduct produces an aviation warning effect.
12. A compound airspace hazard is established when runway drone interference and navigation signal interference are both present.
13. Maintenance window authorization is established when the airside conduct is supported by complete maintenance authorization records.
14. Emergency tarmac authorization is established when the airside conduct is supported by complete medical-diversion rescue records.
15. The airside access breach article covers airside access violation; the prescribed consequence is a fine or short detention.
16. The runway drone interference article covers runway drone interference; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of not more than 3 years.
17. The navigation signal interference article covers navigation signal interference; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 3 and 7 years.
18. The compound airspace hazard article covers compound airspace hazard; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 7 and 15 years.
19. The maintenance window authorization article covers maintenance window authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. The emergency tarmac authorization article covers emergency tarmac authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
21. Where the runway drone interference article and the airside access breach article both apply, the runway drone interference article prevails.
22. Where the navigation signal interference article and the airside access breach article both apply, the navigation signal interference article prevails.
23. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the airside access breach article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
24. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the runway drone interference article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
25. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the navigation signal interference article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
26. The maintenance window authorization article operates as an exception to the airside access breach article when both apply.
27. The maintenance window authorization article operates as an exception to the runway drone interference article when both apply.
28. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the airside access breach article when both apply.
29. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the runway drone interference article when both apply.
30. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the compound airspace hazard article when both apply.
cases.case_text: Quinn entered an airside gate without a badge. The airside area was marked restricted. Quinn piloted a drone above a service road. The runway lights were active. Quinn jammed a tower channel. A pilot warning was received. A medical diversion was announced. Quinn followed the rescue controller's route. Quinn carried the patient transfer form. No escort officer was present. Quinn had no maintenance work order.

cases.rules_text:
1. Prohibited airside entry is present if any one of the following occurs: a person enters without a badge, uses an expired airport badge, or follows a service vehicle through the gate.
2. Restricted airside status is present when the area is marked restricted and no escort officer is present.
3. Runway-area drone operation is present when a person either launches a drone near the runway or pilots one above a service road.
4. Active runway risk is present when the runway lights are active.
5. Signal-interference conduct is present when a person either transmits an unlicensed beacon or jams a tower channel.
6. An aviation warning effect is present when a pilot warning is received.
7. Maintenance authorization records are complete when there is a work order, the person stays within the assigned zone, and exit is logged before closure.
8. Emergency tarmac records are complete when a medical diversion is announced, the rescue route is followed, and the patient transfer form is carried.
9. An airside access violation is established by prohibited airside entry into a restricted airside area.
10. Runway drone interference is established when an airside access violation is combined with runway-area drone operation and active runway risk.
11. Navigation signal interference is established when signal-interference conduct produces an aviation warning effect.
12. A compound airspace hazard is established when runway drone interference and navigation signal interference are both present.
13. Maintenance window authorization is established when the airside conduct is supported by complete maintenance authorization records.
14. Emergency tarmac authorization is established when the airside conduct is supported by complete medical-diversion rescue records.
15. The airside access breach article covers airside access violation; the prescribed consequence is a fine or short detention.
16. The runway drone interference article covers runway drone interference; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of not more than 3 years.
17. The navigation signal interference article covers navigation signal interference; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 3 and 7 years.
18. The compound airspace hazard article covers compound airspace hazard; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 7 and 15 years.
19. The maintenance window authorization article covers maintenance window authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. The emergency tarmac authorization article covers emergency tarmac authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
21. Where the runway drone interference article and the airside access breach article both apply, the runway drone interference article prevails.
22. Where the navigation signal interference article and the airside access breach article both apply, the navigation signal interference article prevails.
23. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the airside access breach article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
24. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the runway drone interference article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
25. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the navigation signal interference article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
26. The maintenance window authorization article operates as an exception to the airside access breach article when both apply.
27. The maintenance window authorization article operates as an exception to the runway drone interference article when both apply.
28. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the airside access breach article when both apply.
29. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the runway drone interference article when both apply.
30. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the compound airspace hazard article when both apply.

Drew jammed a tower channel. A pilot warning was received. No escort officer was present. Drew had no maintenance work order. No medical diversion was announced.

3. Runway-area drone operation is present when a person either launches a drone near the runway or pilots one above a service road.
4. Active runway risk is present when the runway lights are active.
5. Signal-interference conduct is present when a person either transmits an unlicensed beacon or jams a tower channel.
6. An aviation warning effect is present when a pilot warning is received.
7. Maintenance authorization records are complete when there is a work order, the person stays within the assigned zone, and exit is logged before closure.
8. Emergency tarmac records are complete when a medical diversion is announced, the rescue route is followed, and the patient transfer form is carried.
9. An airside access violation is established by prohibited airside entry into a restricted airside area.
10. Runway drone interference is established when an airside access violation is combined with runway-area drone operation and active runway risk.
11. Navigation signal interference is established when signal-interference conduct produces an aviation warning effect.

14. Emergency tarmac authorization is established when the airside conduct is supported by complete medical-diversion rescue records.

2. Restricted airside status is present when the area is marked restricted and no escort officer is present.
3. Runway-area drone operation is present when a person either launches a drone near the runway or pilots one above a service road.
4. Active runway risk is present when the runway lights are active.
5. Signal-interference conduct is present when a person either transmits an unlicensed beacon or jams a tower channel.
6. An aviation warning effect is present when a pilot warning is received.
7. Maintenance authorization records are complete when there is a work order, the person stays within the assigned zone, and exit is logged before closure.
8. Emergency tarmac records are complete when a medical diversion is announced, the rescue route is followed, and the patient transfer form is carried.
9. An airside access violation is established by prohibited airside entry into a restricted airside area.
10. Runway drone interference is established when an airside access violation is combined with runway-area drone operation and active runway risk.
11. Navigation signal interference is established when signal-interference conduct produces an aviation warning effect.
12. A compound airspace hazard is established when runway drone interference and navigation signal interference are both present.
13. Maintenance window authorization is established when the airside conduct is supported by complete maintenance authorization records.
14. Emergency tarmac authorization is established when the airside conduct is supported by complete medical-diversion rescue records.

cases.rules_text:
1. Prohibited airside entry is present if any one of the following occurs: a person enters without a badge, uses an expired airport badge, or follows a service vehicle through the gate.
2. Restricted airside status is present when the area is marked restricted and no escort officer is present.
3. Runway-area drone operation is present when a person either launches a drone near the runway or pilots one above a service road.
4. Active runway risk is present when the runway lights are active.
5. Signal-interference conduct is present when a person either transmits an unlicensed beacon or jams a tower channel.
6. An aviation warning effect is present when a pilot warning is received.
7. Maintenance authorization records are complete when there is a work order, the person stays within the assigned zone, and exit is logged before closure.
8. Emergency tarmac records are complete when a medical diversion is announced, the rescue route is followed, and the patient transfer form is carried.
9. An airside access violation is established by prohibited airside entry into a restricted airside area.
10. Runway drone interference is established when an airside access violation is combined with runway-area drone operation and active runway risk.
11. Navigation signal interference is established when signal-interference conduct produces an aviation warning effect.
12. A compound airspace hazard is established when runway drone interference and navigation signal interference are both present.
13. Maintenance window authorization is established when the airside conduct is supported by complete maintenance authorization records.
14. Emergency tarmac authorization is established when the airside conduct is supported by complete medical-diversion rescue records.
15. The airside access breach article covers airside access violation; the prescribed consequence is a fine or short detention.
16. The runway drone interference article covers runway drone interference; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of not more than 3 years.
17. The navigation signal interference article covers navigation signal interference; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 3 and 7 years.
18. The compound airspace hazard article covers compound airspace hazard; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 7 and 15 years.
19. The maintenance window authorization article covers maintenance window authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. The emergency tarmac authorization article covers emergency tarmac authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
21. Where the runway drone interference article and the airside access breach article both apply, the runway drone interference article prevails.
22. Where the navigation signal interference article and the airside access breach article both apply, the navigation signal interference article prevails.
23. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the airside access breach article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
24. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the runway drone interference article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
25. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the navigation signal interference article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
26. The maintenance window authorization article operates as an exception to the airside access breach article when both apply.
27. The maintenance window authorization article operates as an exception to the runway drone interference article when both apply.
28. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the airside access breach article when both apply.
29. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the runway drone interference article when both apply.
30. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the compound airspace hazard article when both apply.

Jamie transmitted an unlicensed beacon. A pilot warning was received. Jamie had a maintenance work order. Jamie stayed within the assigned zone. Jamie logged exit before closure. A medical diversion was announced. Jamie followed the rescue controller's route. Jamie carried the patient transfer form. No escort officer was present.

cases.rules_text:
1. Prohibited airside entry is present if any one of the following occurs: a person enters without a badge, uses an expired airport badge, or follows a service vehicle through the gate.
2. Restricted airside status is present when the area is marked restricted and no escort officer is present.
3. Runway-area drone operation is present when a person either launches a drone near the runway or pilots one above a service road.
4. Active runway risk is present when the runway lights are active.
5. Signal-interference conduct is present when a person either transmits an unlicensed beacon or jams a tower channel.
6. An aviation warning effect is present when a pilot warning is received.
7. Maintenance authorization records are complete when there is a work order, the person stays within the assigned zone, and exit is logged before closure.
8. Emergency tarmac records are complete when a medical diversion is announced, the rescue route is followed, and the patient transfer form is carried.
9. An airside access violation is established by prohibited airside entry into a restricted airside area.
10. Runway drone interference is established when an airside access violation is combined with runway-area drone operation and active runway risk.
11. Navigation signal interference is established when signal-interference conduct produces an aviation warning effect.
12. A compound airspace hazard is established when runway drone interference and navigation signal interference are both present.
13. Maintenance window authorization is established when the airside conduct is supported by complete maintenance authorization records.
14. Emergency tarmac authorization is established when the airside conduct is supported by complete medical-diversion rescue records.
15. The airside access breach article covers airside access violation; the prescribed consequence is a fine or short detention.
16. The runway drone interference article covers runway drone interference; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of not more than 3 years.
17. The navigation signal interference article covers navigation signal interference; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 3 and 7 years.
18. The compound airspace hazard article covers compound airspace hazard; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 7 and 15 years.
19. The maintenance window authorization article covers maintenance window authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. The emergency tarmac authorization article covers emergency tarmac authorization; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
21. Where the runway drone interference article and the airside access breach article both apply, the runway drone interference article prevails.
22. Where the navigation signal interference article and the airside access breach article both apply, the navigation signal interference article prevails.
23. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the airside access breach article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
24. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the runway drone interference article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
25. Where the compound airspace hazard article and the navigation signal interference article both apply, the compound airspace hazard article prevails.
26. The maintenance window authorization article operates as an exception to the airside access breach article when both apply.
27. The maintenance window authorization article operates as an exception to the runway drone interference article when both apply.
28. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the airside access breach article when both apply.
29. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the runway drone interference article when both apply.
30. The emergency tarmac authorization article operates as an exception to the compound airspace hazard article when both apply.
